---
title: "dsci445termProject.rmd"
author: "Mark Gardner"
date: "11/9/2021"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(GGally)
library(ISLR)
library(class)
library(MASS)
library(dplyr)
library(gbm) ## boosting package
library(randomForest) 
library(glmnet)
library(modelr)
library(rpart)
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
library(ISLR) ## data package
library(tidyverse) ## data manipulation
library(knitr) ## tables
library(tree)
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
# realData <- read_csv("train.csv")
realData <- read_csv("realEstate.csv")
head(realData)
colnames(realData)
re <- realData
# get rid of useless columns
re <- re[,c(-(1:3),-7,-12,-18,-27,-29,-(31:68))]
# remove observations with missing data
 # two choices: omit everything with na, or create mean for thar var later. 
#######re <- na.omit(re)
#ggpairs(re)
#colnames(realData)
#colnames(re)
#######realData <- re
#colnames(realData)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
library(ISLR) ## data package
library(tidyverse) ## data manipulation
library(knitr) ## tables
#p <- ggplot(data = realData, mapping = aes(x=TotRmsAbvGrd, y = SalePrice, color = MSZoning)) + 
  #geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
#  geom_point(alpha=.3)+ 
#  scale_x_log10()+
###  scale_y_continuous()+
#  facet_wrap(~MSZoning)
#colnames(re)
#p <- ggplot(data = realData, mapping = aes(x=TotRmsAbvGrd, y = SalePrice, color = MSZoning)) + 
  #geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
#  geom_point(alpha=.3)+ 
#  scale_x_log10()+
#  scale_y_continuous()+
#  facet_wrap(~MSZoning)
#p
#CORRECT 
#names(realData)
#summary(realData)
library(tree)
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
#summary(realData$SalePrice)
#colnames(realData)
```

```{r}
# realData2 <- realData %>% 
#  mutate(high_sales = factor(ifelse(SalePrice > 214000, "high" , "low")))
# address and description; raw; don't include 
#summary(realData)
colnames(realData2)
#realData2 <- realData
realData2 <- re
#re$`Lot Size`
########head(realData2$Price)
#is.numeric(realData2$Price)
# db <- gsub(",","",db)
#realData2$Price <- gsub("$", "", realData2$Price)
#realData3$Price <- gsub(",", "", realData2$Price)
########realData2$Price <- gsub(",", "", realData2$Price)
########separatedPrice <- parse_number(realData2$Price)
########realData2$SqrFt <- gsub(",", "", realData2$SqrFt)
#str_replace(realData2$Price, "[$]", "")
#realData2$Price <- as.numeric((realData2$Price))
#realData2$Price <- substring(2, realData2$Price)
#summary(realData2$Price)
########head(realData2$Price)
########realData2$Price <- realData2$Price %>% 
########  str_replace("[$]", "")
########realData2$SqrFt <- realData2$SqrFt %>% 
########  str_replace(" sqft", "")
#is.numeric(realData2$SqrFt)
#is.character(realData2$SqrFt)
########realData2$SqrFt <- as.numeric(realData2$SqrFt)
# NAs introduced; how to create average of that missing value? 
# should long and lat be numeric? character? factor? 
# lotSize 
#hairdo %>% mutate(colour = ifelse(str_detect(hair, "Long."), "Blond", "Brunette"))
# realData2$lotSize
#realData2$newLotSize <- c()
#realData2CorrectLotSize <- realData2 %>% mutate(realData2$newLotSize, ifelse(str_detect(lotSize, "acres"), as.numeric(realData2$lotSize) * 43560, lotSize))
#realData2CorrectLotSize$
########realData2$lotSize <- realData2$lotSize %>% 
########  str_replace(" sqft", "") 
########is.numeric(realData2$lotSize)
########is.character(realData2$lotSize)
########realData2$lotSize <- as.numeric(realData2$lotSize)
# from sam's 
# from sam's 
#inSqft <- rep(NA,length(realData2$lotSize))

# sam's 
#inSqft <- rep(NA,length(re$lotSize))
#for (i in 1:length(re$lotSize)) {
#  if (grepl("sqft",re$lotSize[i],fixed=TRUE)) {
#    inSqft[i] <- TRUE
##  } else {
#    inSqft[i] <- FALSE
#  }
#}
# 
realData2 <- realData2 %>% 
  mutate(high_sales = factor(ifelse(Price > 400000, "high" , "low"))) 
min(realData2$lotSize)
max(realData2$lotSize)
colnames(realData2)
summary(realData2$high_sales)
# realData2 <- subset(realData2, select = -c(SalePrice))
subset2$price <- separatedPrice
realData2 <- subset(realData2, select = -c(Price))
colnames(realData2)
#realData2 <- subset(realData2, select = -c(3SsnPorch))
#colnames(realData2)[70] <- "thirdSSNPorch"
#colnames(realData2)
#colnames(realData2)[44]
#colnames(realData2)[44] <- "firstFloorSQFT"
#colnames(realData2)[45]
#colnames(realData2)[45] <- "secondFloorSQFT"
#colnames(realData2)
```

```{r}
#######nrow(realData2)
#######ncol(realData2)
# naCols <- vector(length=81)
#######naCols <- vector(length=22)
# for (i in 1:81){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
#######for (i in 1:22){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
#######subset <- realData2[,which(naCols < 10)]
#######ncol(subset)
#######nrow(subset)
#######colnames(subset)
colnames(realData2)
summary(realData2)
colnames(realData2)[5] <- "SqrFt"
colnames(realData2)
colnames(realData2)[5]
colnames(realData2)[8] <- "lotSize"
colnames(realData2)[8]
colnames(realData2)[11] <- "yearBuilt"
colnames(realData2)[11]
colnames(realData2)[12] <- "pricePerSqFoot"
colnames(realData2)[12]
colnames(realData2)[13] <- "lastSoldYear"
colnames(realData2)[13]
colnames(realData2)[14] <- "lastSoldFor"
colnames(realData2)[14]
colnames(realData2)[15] <- "lastTaxAssessment"
colnames(realData2)[15]
colnames(realData2)[16] <- "lastTaxYear"
colnames(realData2)[16]
colnames(realData2)[21] <- "addressFull"
colnames(realData2)[21]
colnames(realData2)[22] <- "dayOnTrulia"
colnames(realData2)[22]
colnames(realData2)

for (i in 1:length(realData2$lotSize)) {
  if (grepl("sqft",realData2$lotSize[i],fixed=TRUE)) {
    #inSqft[i] <- TRUE
    # re$LotSize[i] <- re$LotSize[i]*(1/43560)
    
    realData2$lotSize[i] <- parse_number(realData2$lotSize[i])*(1/43560)
  } else {
    #inSqft[i] <- FALSE
   realData2$lotSize[i] <- parse_number(realData2$lotSize[i])
  }
}
```

```{r}
library(tree)
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(party)
library(partykit)

#####subset2$price <- separatedPrice

```

```{r}

#names(subset2)
#names(realData2)
subset <- realData2
subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title, Description, Address, addressFull, City, State, Zipcode))
#######subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title, Description, Address, addressFull)) #ADD STYLE BACK 
subset2$lastTaxAssessment <- parse_number(subset2$lastTaxAssessment)
# break this into two level factor.
subset2$Style <- parse_number(subset2$Style)
subset2$SqrFt <- parse_number(subset2$SqrFt)
subset2$lotSize <- parse_number(subset2$lotSize)
subset2$pricePerSqFoot <- parse_number(subset2$pricePerSqFoot)
subset2$lastSoldFor <- parse_number(subset2$lastSoldFor)
#subset2$City <- as.factor(subset2$City)
#subset2$State <- as.factor(subset2$State)
#subset2$Zipcode <- as.factor(subset2$Zipcode)
######subset2$Price <- parse_number(subset2$Price)
names(subset2)

subset2$price <- separatedPrice

#TREEE
options(scipen=5)
tree_fit <- tree(Price ~ ., data = subset2)
summary(tree_fit)#USE
plot(tree_fit)
text(tree_fit, use.n=TRUE, cex = .6)
tree_fit$terms
```

```{r}
# RANDOM FOREST 

names(subset2)
n <- nrow(subset2)
n
#n <- floor(.8 * nrow(subset))
n <- floor(.8 * nrow(subset2))
n
#train_ind <- sample(seq_len(nrow(subset)),  size = n)
train_ind <- sample(seq_len(nrow(subset2)),  size = n)
#####train_subset <- subset[train_ind,]
#####nrow(train_subset)
#####test_subset <- subset[-train_ind,]
#####nrow(test_subset)
#####class(test_subset)
#test <- -train_ind
#####length(test)
###### subset2
names(subset2)
train_subset <- subset2[train_ind,]
nrow(train_subset)
ncol(train_subset)
ncol(test_subset)
test_subset <- subset2[-train_ind,]
nrow(test_subset)
ncol(test_subset)
#
#####naCols <- vector(length=14)
#####for (i in 1:14){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(train_subset[,i]))}
#####train_subset_2 <- train_subset[,which(naCols < 10)]
#####naCols <- vector(length=14)
#####for (i in 1:14){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(test_subset[,i]))}
#####test_subset_2 <- test_subset[,which(naCols < 10)]

#4th try 
# COREECT BELOW 
train_subset3 <- train_subset %>%
          mutate_if(is.character, factor)

test_subset3 <- test_subset %>%
          mutate_if(is.character, factor)

test_subset3$high_sales

ctrl_none <- trainControl(method = "none")

names(train_subset3)
# https://therbootcamp.github.io/appliedML_2019Jan/_sessions/Prediction/Prediction_practical.html
# done below 
# test 

mvc <- 0 
for(i in 1:ncol(test_subset)){
  m = sum(is.na(test_subset[,i]))
  print(paste("column ", colnames(test_subset[i]), " has ", m , "missing values"))
  if(m>0){
    mvc = mvc+1
  }else{
    mvc
  }
}
#test_subset4 <- test_subset3    
test_subset4 <- na.aggregate(test_subset)
names(test_subset4)

mvc <- 0 
for(i in 1:ncol(test_subset4)){
  m = sum(is.na(test_subset4[,i]))
  print(paste("column ", colnames(test_subset4[i]), " has ", m , "missing values"))
  if(m>0){
    mvc = mvc+1
  }else{
    mvc
  }
}



# train 
mvc <- 0 
for(i in 1:ncol(train_subset)){
  m = sum(is.na(train_subset[,i]))
  print(paste("column ", colnames(train_subset[i]), " has ", m , "missing values"))
  if(m>0){
    mvc = mvc+1
  }else{
    mvc
  }
}
#train_subset4 <- train_subset3    
train_subset4 <- na.aggregate(train_subset)

mvc <- 0 
for(i in 1:ncol(train_subset4)){
  m = sum(is.na(train_subset4[,i]))
  print(paste("column ", colnames(train_subset4[i]), " has ", m , "missing values"))
  if(m>0){
    mvc = mvc+1
  }else{
    mvc
  }
}
install.packages("zoo")
library(zoo)
#######data2 <- data                             # Duplicate data frame

library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
#######Mode <- function(x) {
#######  ux <- unique(x)
#######  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
#######}
#######train_subset4 <- train_subset3 %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, funs(replace(.,is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE)))) %>%
#######  mutate_if(is.factor, funs(replace(.,is.na(.), Mode(na.omit(.)))))

# done above 
# Duplicate data frame
#######train_subset4$price
#for(i in 1:ncol(data)) {         # Replace NA in all columns
#######for(i in 1:ncol(train_subset4)) {      # Replace NA in all columns
#######  train_subset4[ , i][is.na(train_subset4[ , i])] <- mean(train_subset4[ , i], na.rm = TRUE)
#######}
#######train_subset4

#out.rf <- randomForest(price~.,
#                       data = train_subset3, importance=T)
out.rf <- randomForest(Price~.,
                       data = train_subset4, importance=T)
#summary(out.rf)

#print(out.rf)
importance(out.rf)
fitForest1 <- predict(out.rf, newdata = test_subset4)
fitForest1[1:10]
```

```{r}
importance <- importance(out.rf)
varImportance <- data.frame(Variables = row.names(importance),
                            Importance = round(importance[, '%IncMSE'],2))
rankImportance <- varImportance %>% mutate(Rank = paste0('#', dense_rank(desc(Importance))))

print("Plot of variable importance")
print("Variable importance of initial model")
ggplot(rankImportance, aes(x = reorder(Variables, Importance), 
                           y = Importance, fill = Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat='identity') + 
  geom_text(aes(x = Variables, y = 0.5, label = Rank),
            hjust=0, vjust=0.55, size = 4, colour = 'red') +
  labs(x = 'Variables') +
  coord_flip() 

```

```{r}
first_SSE <- mean((fitForest1-test_subset4$Price)^2)
first_SSE
mean(train_subset4$Price)
print("Inital prediction")
#testy <- test_subset4[test_subset4$Price != max(test_subset4$Price)]
plot(fitForest1,test_subset4$Price,
     xlab="predicted",ylab="actual", xaxt="n")
abline(a=0,b=1)

```

```{r}
ctrl_none <- trainControl(method = "none")

grad_rf <- train(form = Price ~ .,   
                 data = train_subset4,
                 method = "rf",
                 trControl = ctrl_none,
                 tuneGrid = expand.grid(mtry = 2), 
                 na.action=na.roughfix)
#test_subset5 <- na.omit(test_subset3)
#price_rf_fit <- predict(grad_rf, newdata = test_subset3)
price_rf_fit <- predict(grad_rf, newdata = test_subset4)


```

```{r}
#diff btwn bag and forest is sqrt(ncol(train_subset4) -1)
bag_fit <- randomForest(Price ~ ., data = train_subset4, mtry = ncol(train_subset4) - 1, importance = TRUE)
#
#####data.frame(bag_fit$importance )%>%
#####  mutate(variable = rownames(bag_fit$importance)) %>%
#####  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = variable[order(MeanDecreaseGini)])) %>% ## trick to plot variable by descending Gini
#####  ggplot() +
#####  geom_point(aes(MeanDecreaseGini, variable))
#
predict_bag_fit_price <- predict(bag_fit, newdata = test_subset4)
predict_bag_fit_price[1:10]

```

```{r}
# http://rstudio-pubs-static.s3.amazonaws.com/156481_80ee6ee3a0414fd38f5d3ad33d14c771.html
#boost_fit <- gbm(Price ~ ., train_subs, n.trees = 5000, shrinkage = 0.1, interaction.depth = 2, distribution = "bernoulli")

boostPrice <- gbm(Price~.,train_subset4,distribution="gaussian",n.trees=5000,interaction.depth=4)
summary(boostPrice)

predict_boost_fit_price <- predict(boostPrice, newdata = test_subset4)

#predict_boost_fit_price[1:10]




par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(boostPrice,i="pricePerSqFoot")
plot(boostPrice,i="SqrFt")
ggplot(boost)
# distribution skewed ot the west, Californian influence? 
fitForest1[1:9]
price_rf_fit[1:9]
train_subset4$Price[1:9]
predict_bag_fit_price[1:9]
predict_boost_fit_price[1:9]
```

```{r}
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
fit_poly_7 <- lm(Price ~ poly(SqrFt, 7), data = train_subset4)
summary(fit_poly_7)

fit_poly <- list()
d <- 7 # highest degree to check
for(i in seq_len(d)) {
  fit_poly_7[[i]] <- lm(Price ~ poly(SqrFt, 7), data = train_subset4)
}
do.call(anova, fit_poly_7)ss
```

```{r}
 # CORRECT ABOVE 
#3rd tru
library(rpart)
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
subset2$price <- separatedPrice
fit <- rpart(price ~ lotSize + SqrFt + pricePerSqFoot + lastSoldYear + lastSoldFor + Latitude + Longitude + Beds + Bath, data = subset2)
plot(fit, uniform=TRUE)
text(fit, cex = 0.6)

names(subset2)
predict(fit, head(subset2))
print(head(subset2$price))

library(modelr)
library(rpart)
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
splitData <- resample_partition(subset2, c(test=0.3, train = 0.7))

fit3 <- randomForest(price ~ lotSize + SqrFt + pricePerSqFoot + lastSoldYear + lastSoldFor + Latitude + Longitude + Beds + Bath, data = splitData$train, na.action = na.exclude)

predict(fit3, head(subset2))
print(head(subset2$price))

# 2nd try

####naCols <- vector(length=22)
# for (i in 1:81){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
####for (i in 1:22){ naCols[i]<- sum(is.na(realData2[,i]))}
####subset <- realData2[,which(naCols < 10)]
#train <- sample(seq_len(n), 6500)
#length(train)
#test <- -train
#length(test)
#train_subset <- subset[train,]
tree_fit_final <- tree(high_sales ~ ., train_subset)
tree_fit_final <- tree(Price ~ ., train_subset4)
summary(tree_fit_final)
#confusion <- table(pred = predict(tree_fit_final, subset[test,], type = "class"), true = subset[test,]$high_sales)
# does this make sense? WHat am I predicting? 
confusion <- table(pred = predict(tree_fit_final, test_subset4, type = "vector"), true = test_subset4$Price)
(confusion[1, 2] + confusion[2, 1])/sum(confusion)
confusion[1, 1] 
confusion[1, 2]
confusion
```


```{r}
# removing unneeded stuff; creating as.factor and as.numeric. 
names(subset)
names(subset2)
subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title))
subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title, Description, Address, addressFull, City, State, Zipcode, Style)) #ADD STYLE BACK 
subset2 <- subset(subset, select = -c(Title, Description, Address, addressFull, City)) #ADD STYLE BACK 
subset2 <- subset(subset2, select = -c(high_sales))
names(subset2)
subset2$lotSize
subset2$lastTaxAssessment <- parse_number(subset2$lastTaxAssessment)
# break this into two level factor.
subset2$Style <- as.factor(subset2$Style)
subset2$SqrFt <- parse_number(subset2$SqrFt)
subset2$lotSize <- parse_number(subset2$lotSize)
subset2$pricePerSqFoot <- parse_number(subset2$pricePerSqFoot)
subset2$lastSoldFor <- parse_number(subset2$lastSoldFor)
subset2$City <- as.factor(subset2$City)
subset2$State <- as.factor(subset2$State)
subset2$Zipcode <- as.factor(subset2$Zipcode)
tree_cv <- cv.tree(tree_fit_final, FUN = prune.misclass)
tree_cv$size # What does this mean? 
prune_fit <- prune.misclass(tree_fit, k = tree_cv$k[which.min(tree_cv$dev)])
library(glmnet)
library(modelr)
library(rpart)
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
# One error we see here: low predictive power for high valuation; too few observations. Monte carlo ? 
#confusion_prune <- table(pred = predict(prune_fit, subset[test,], type = "class"), true = subset[test,]$high_sales)
confusion_prune <- table(pred = predict(tree_fit_final, test_subset, type = "class"), true = test_subset$high_sales)
confusion_prune
(confusion_prune[1, 2] + confusion_prune[2, 1])/sum(confusion_prune)
# This one performs better by about .02 . 
library(randomForest) 
library(glmnet)
library(modelr)
library(rpart)
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
# check all variables; make char to int or decimal where nececssary. ; make all char to factor if appropriate. 
summary(subset)
bag_fit2 <- randomForest(Price ~ ., data = train_subset4, mtry = ncol(train_subset) - 1, importance = TRUE)
data.frame(bag_fit2$importance )%>%
  mutate(variable = rownames(bag_fit2$importance)) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = variable[order(MeanDecreaseGini)])) %>% ## trick to plot variable by descending Gini
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(MeanDecreaseGini, variable))
  # latitute = california or the north? 
```

```{r}
confusion_bag <- table(pred = predict(bag_fit, test_subset_2, type = "class"), true = test_subset_2$high_sales)
# Some weird, unknown or uninitialized column error 
(confusion_bag[1, 2] + confusion_bag[2, 1])/sum(confusion_bag)
rf_fit <- randomForest(high_sales ~ ., data = train_subset,  mtry = sqrt(ncol(train_subset) - 1), importance = TRUE)
rf_fit <- randomForest(high_sales ~ ., data = trn_df, mtry = sqrt(ncol(trn_df) - 1), importance = TRUE)
data.frame(rf_fit$importance)%>%
  mutate(variable = rownames(rf_fit$importance)) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = variable[order(MeanDecreaseGini)])) %>% ## trick to plot variable by descending Gini
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(MeanDecreaseGini, variable)) 
train_subset_2$
test_subset_2$
# PROBLEM: get predicted high/low and prices for that subset; not original separated prices 
# separatedPrice
summary(bag_fit)
bag_fit$predicted
bag_fit$terms
bag_fit$forest
```

```{r}
confusion_rf <- table(pred = predict(rf_fit, test_subset_2, type = "class"), true = test_subset_2$high_sales)
(confusion_rf[1, 2] + confusion_rf[2, 1])/sum(confusion_rf)
```

```{r}
confusion_rf
```
```{r}
c(confusion_rf[2, 1], confusion_rf[1, 2])/colSums(confusion_rf)
```
```{r}
rf_fit$confusion
```

```{r}
#boosting 
library(gbm) ## boosting package
library(randomForest) 
library(glmnet)
library(modelr)
library(rpart)
library(splines)
library(zoo)
library(dplyr)
subset2$high_sales
subset_boost$high_sales <- subset2 %>% 
  mutate(subset2, high_sales = ifelse(high_sales = "high", 1, 0))
  
subset_2_boost$high_sales
subset_2_boost <- subset2 %>%
  mutate(high_sales = 2 - as.numeric(high_sales))
train_subset_2_boost <- train_subset_2 %>%
  mutate(high_sales = 2 - as.numeric(high_sales))
boost_fit <- gbm(high_sales ~ ., train_subset_2_boost, n.trees = 5000, shrinkage = 0.1, interaction.depth = 2, distribution = "bernoulli")
confusion_boost <- table(pred = predict(boost_fit, train_subset_2, type = "response") > 0.5, true = train_subset_2$high_sales)
(confusion_boost[1, 2] + confusion_boost[2, 1])/sum(confusion_boost)
confusion_boost
c(confusion_boost[2, 1], confusion_boost[1, 2])/colSums(confusion_boost)
```

```{r}
realData[70]
is.data.frame(realData2)
names(realData2[70]) <- "thirdSSNPorch"
typeof(realData2)
df <- as.data.frame(realData2)
df[70]           
typeof(df)
realData2 <- realData2 %>% 
  rename(3SsnPorch = thirdSSNPorch)
# CORRECT 
df <- Carseats
df <- df %>%
  mutate(high_sales = factor(ifelse(Sales > 8, "high", "low"))) %>%
  select(-Sales)
tree_fit <- tree(high_sales ~ ., realData2)
```

```{r}
lm <- lm(SalePrice ~ Id + MSSubClass + MSZoning , data = realData)
lm <- lm(SalePrice ~  ., data = realData[,values_count > 1])
summary(lm)
summary(realData)
(l <- sapply(realData, function(x) is.factor(x)))
m <- realData[, l]
m
ifelse(n <- sapply(m, function(x) length(levels(x))) == 1, "DROP", "NODROP")
n
values_count <- sapply(lapply(realData, unique), length)
names(realData)
p0 <- ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x=carat, y = price, color = color)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  #geom_point(alpha=.5)
# p
p <- ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x=carat, y = price, color = color)) + 
  #geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  geom_point(alpha=.1)
p0 <- ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x=carat, y = price, color = color)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = color)) 
  #geom_point(alpha=.5)
# p
ggpairs(realData)
head(realData)
cols = colnames(realData)
for (col in cols){
  if(is.factor(realData[[col]])){
    cat(col, ' has ', length(levels(realData[[col]])), '\n')
  }
}
summary(realData)
```

## Including Plots 

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
